reasoning: The ICE BofA Euro High Yield Index effective yield ended at 5.10% on 2025-11-07, following a sharp rise from 4.96% in late October. The FRED linear trend forecast implies a slight downward pull toward 5.07% over the coming week (P=0.44 of an increase), likely reflecting short-term mean reversion. Over the medium term, the broader macroeconomic backdrop features the European Central Bank continuing an easing cycle, which generally exerts downward pressure on euro-denominated yields, barring a recessionary spike in credit spreads. Consequently, I expect the yield is slightly more likely to be lower than the current 5.10% basis. Strict adherence to the forecasting rule that uncertainty must increase over time—tethering longer-horizon forecasts closer to 0.50—dictates probabilities that pull progressively toward 50%.
